code_change: Expanded method/action and operand role vocab for graph traversal methods/state names; added missing_graph_walk_evidence action-trace residual and replay token signals.

eval_targets.score_semantics: Weights admitted private/licensed target-body token positions for the prefix-conditioned body-transition auxiliary head. The labels are the same target next tokens used for normal supervised training, masked to body-continuation positions after SLOT:BODY_START when present. This trains a learned prefix-conditioned projection only; it does not render code, inspect tests/solutions, use public benchmarks, or grant learned-generation credit.

evidence.score_semantics: Private prefix-conditioned body-transition auxiliary supervision. It trains a separate learned projection from source plus generated target prefix to the next body token over admitted private/licensed target-body continuation positions. It emits no candidate by itself and grants no learned-generation credit without strict decode/verifier behavior.

train_targets.score_semantics: Weights admitted private/licensed target-body token positions for the prefix-conditioned body-transition auxiliary head. The labels are the same target next tokens used for normal supervised training, masked to body-continuation positions after SLOT:BODY_START when present. This trains a learned prefix-conditioned projection only; it does not render code, inspect tests/solutions, use public benchmarks, or grant learned-generation credit.

eval_targets.score_semantics: Maps admitted private/licensed target-body tokens onto broad executable action roles such as return, branch, block exit, update operator, expression closure, and identifier. The active mask is inherited from the private body-transition target mask. This creates a trainable structural next-action objective; it does not render code, inspect tests or solutions, use public benchmark data, or grant learned-generation credit.

evidence.score_semantics: Private prefix-conditioned body-action auxiliary supervision. It trains a learned structural role head over body-continuation positions, with roles such as return, branch, block exit, update operator, and expression closure. It emits no candidate by itself and grants no learned-generation credit without strict decode/verifier behavior.

train_targets.score_semantics: Maps admitted private/licensed target-body tokens onto broad executable action roles such as return, branch, block exit, update operator, expression closure, and identifier. The active mask is inherited from the private body-transition target mask. This creates a trainable structural next-action objective; it does not render code, inspect tests or solutions, use public benchmark data, or grant learned-generation credit.

interpretation: Graph-aware role mapping is wired into the existing action/operand target path and produces local_state/method_name signal.
The one-epoch smoke improved operand heldout loss/accuracy but worsened LM, transition, and action heldout losses, so the checkpoint is rejected.
The next patch should use these graph/operation residuals in a better state-machine/localized semantic-IR body constructor or tune multi-loss balance before any decode replay from this checkpoint.
next_required_work: Do not route this checkpoint. Balance or redesign the body-action/transition objectives and then train a candidate that improves heldout LM plus action/transition/operand heads before strict private replay.
no_claim_boundary: This smoke is not a learned-generation capability win. The checkpoint is rejected because hard heldout LM loss worsened; no public rows, runtime external inference, fallback, template, router, tool, or deterministic repair credit is claimed.
purpose: Verify graph-aware body action/operand role mapping is consumed by the existing trainable strict-generator path without creating a new candidate family.
